title: parinari littoralis
intro: Parinari littoralis, also known as the coastal breadfruit, is a tropical plant species native to the coastal regions of Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where the plant's leaves and roots are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as reduce swelling and pain. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Parinari littoralis can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
